next_steps: Perform microscopic skin scrapings for scabies mites, eggs, or fecal pellets
Obtain a rapid plasma reagin (RPR) and treponemal-specific test for syphilis
Conduct a full physical examination focusing on skin, mucosal, and genital surfaces
Apply permethrin 5% cream empirically for suspected scabies infestation
Ensure standard and contact precautions are in place
Obtain a detailed medication history to assess for potential drug-induced rash

differential_diagnoses: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS)
next_steps: Perform RPR and treponemal-specific testing for syphilis
Swab the oral ulcer for HSV and VZV PCR
Perform microscopic skin scraping for scabies mites
Conduct a thorough physical examination including genital and mucosal areas
Obtain blood cultures and repeat CBC with differential to monitor lymphocyte count
Initiate contact precautions pending definitive diagnosis
Apply permethrin 5% cream only after infectious disease consultations confirm suspicion of scabies
